title: Delightfully Dense Polish Barley Pilaf
description: A malty, fluffy, and indulgent Polish dish featuring all-purpose seasoning, this pilaf is a perfect comfort food for any occasion, and its ingredients are suitable for a Buddhist temple gathering.
instructions: In a large copper pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the grated carrot and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the pearl barley and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the water, all-purpose seasoning, salt, and black pepper, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 40 minutes, or until the barley is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it rest, covered, for 10 minutes.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and serve hot.
